title: GEODIST
description: Returns the distance between two members of a geospatial index.
syntax: GEODIST key member1 member2 [M | KM | FT | MI]

redis_py.signature: geodist(name: KeyT, place1: FieldT, place2: FieldT, unit: Optional[str] = None)

jedis.signature: Double geodist(final byte[] key, final byte[] member1, final byte[] member2)

params.description: can be M, KM, MI or FT can M, KM, MI or FT
params.name: unit

params.description: can be M, KM, MI or FT can M, KM, MI or FT
params.name: unit

params.description: the key of the geo set.
params.name: key

returns.description: distance between points from and to. If one or more elements are missing null is returned.

returns.description: distance in specified unit if both members present, nil otherwise

content:
Return the distance between two members in the geospatial index represented by the sorted set. Given a sorted set representing a geospatial index, populated using the [`GEOADD`]() command, the command returns the distance between the two specified members in the specified unit. If one or both the members are missing, the command returns NULL. The unit must be one of the following, and defaults to meters: * **m** for meters. * **km** for kilometers. * **mi** for miles. * **ft** for feet. The distance is computed assuming that the Earth is a perfect sphere, so errors up to 0.5% are possible in edge cases. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} GEOADD Sicily 13.361389 38.115556 "Palermo" 15.087269 37.502669 "Catania" GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania km GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania mi GEODIST Sicily Foo Bar {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="geodist-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Nil reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): one or both of the elements are missing. * [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): distance as a double (represented as a string) in the specified units. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Null reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#nulls): one or both of the elements are missing. * [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): distance as a double (represented as a string) in the specified units. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
GEOADD Sicily 13.361389 38.115556 "Palermo" 15.087269 37.502669 "Catania"
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania km
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania mi
GEODIST Sicily Foo Bar
intro:
Return the distance between two members in the geospatial index represented by the sorted set.

Given a sorted set representing a geospatial index, populated using the [`GEOADD`]() command, the command returns the distance between the two specified members in the specified unit.

If one or both the members are missing, the command returns NULL.

The unit must be one of the following, and defaults to meters:

* **m** for meters.
* **km** for kilometers.
* **mi** for miles.
* **ft** for feet.

The distance is computed assuming that the Earth is a perfect sphere, so errors up to 0.5% are possible in edge cases.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Return the distance between two members in the geospatial index represented by the sorted set.

Given a sorted set representing a geospatial index, populated using the [`GEOADD`]() command, the command returns the distance between the two specified members in the specified unit.

If one or both the members are missing, the command returns NULL.

The unit must be one of the following, and defaults to meters:

* **m** for meters.
* **km** for kilometers.
* **mi** for miles.
* **ft** for feet.

The distance is computed assuming that the Earth is a perfect sphere, so errors up to 0.5% are possible in edge cases.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
GEOADD Sicily 13.361389 38.115556 "Palermo" 15.087269 37.502669 "Catania"
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania km
GEODIST Sicily Palermo Catania mi
GEODIST Sicily Foo Bar
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="geodist-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Nil reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): one or both of the elements are missing.
* [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): distance as a double (represented as a string) in the specified units.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Null reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#nulls): one or both of the elements are missing.
* [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): distance as a double (represented as a string) in the specified units.

{{< /multitabs >}}
